assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

(Reassessment â€” supersedes the stale matrix cell `daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready` / "no model change detection between turns per parser exploration", which predates the pi parser's model_change handling.)

## Primitive

Per-turn model selection: the user changes pi's active model between turns (REPL `/model` command), and each subsequent assistant turn's usage metadata reflects the new model. The dashboard's ModelName updates to the latest model; TotalTokens keeps accumulating across the switch.

## agent_supports â€” yes

pi 0.70.0's REPL `/model <ref>` command switches the active model in-session WITHOUT starting a new session â€” verified live: one rollout file, two assistant turns, the second attributed to the switch target. Crucially `/model <provider>/<id>` (canonical form) resolves an EXACT single-model match and calls session.setModel() DIRECTLY â€” no arrow-key picker, no interactive selector (dist/modes/interactive/interactive-mode.js handleModelCommand â†’ findExactModelMatch; dist/core/model-resolver.js findExactModelReferenceMatch). So the switch is driveable from a non-interactive script via the existing `slash` step; no `keys` primitive is required.

## irrlicht_observes â€” yes

pi writes the model in two parser-observable places (core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/parser.go): every assistant message carries it at message.model (L103-105 â†’ ev.ModelName), and an explicit model_change event carries it at modelId (L39-44 â†’ ev.ModelName, ev.Skip=true). The tailer applies the model metadata even on the Skip'd model_change line. ev.ModelName flows into SessionMetrics.ModelName under MergeMetrics' latest-non-unknown-wins rule, so by the end of turn 2 ModelName == model B. extractPiContribution (L148) tags each per-turn contribution with that turn's model, so TotalTokens accumulates across both. Live rollout confirmed the sequence: model_change(A) â†’ user â†’ assistant(model=A,stop) â†’ model_change(B) â†’ user â†’ assistant(model=B,stop).

## Observability caveat â€” where the model assertion lives

The expected.jsonl validator DSL (tools/agent-onboarding/internal/validate Phase struct: phase / expected_state / kind / max_delay_ms / duration_at_least_ms / same_session_as / new_session / invariants) operates over the lifecycle events.jsonl stream, which carries NO model_name field. So expected.jsonl cannot directly assert model Aâ†’B. It asserts the two-turn lifecycle skeleton on ONE UUID rollout (born ready â†’ t1 workingâ†’ready â†’ t2 workingâ†’ready, no waiting, one process_exited at teardown). The model-switch proof (model_name == model B at end, distinct from model A) is the metrics-level summary.model_name in the .replay.json.golden (diffed by replay-fixtures) plus the pi parser unit tests on message.model / model_change extraction.

## Conclusion

applicable: yes. SCRIPT mode is recordable via the inline-arg `/model <provider>/<id>` form (no picker). The cell captures two no-op turns separated by a direct model switch; the model Aâ†’B change is documented in spec notes and asserted at the golden + parser-test level, since the events.jsonl DSL has no model field.
caveats: The concrete model values are environment-dependent (pi's boot default model + whichever switch target is authenticated on the account). Per issue #440 the durable assertion is that model_name is present and CHANGES between turns, not a hardcoded pair â€” but to keep the recording deterministic the recipe pins both: launch on the boot default (openai-codex/gpt-5.5 here) and switch to a second authenticated model (openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini) via the canonical provider/id form.

`/model <provider>/<id>` switches DIRECTLY only when the reference resolves to exactly one model. A fuzzy/partial term (e.g. `/model mini` matching several) falls back to the interactive picker â€” so the recipe MUST use the full canonical provider/id form to stay script-driveable.

The model switch lives at metrics level (SessionMetrics.ModelName), not in the events.jsonl lifecycle stream the expected.jsonl DSL operates over â€” so the DSL spec asserts only the two-turn lifecycle arc; the Aâ†’B model claim is covered by the replay golden's summary.model_name plus parser unit tests.

Driver counter quirk: the driver's `slash` step bumps the expected-turn counter like `send`, but a `/model` switch produces a model_change line with NO assistant turn. So a `wait_turn` placed AFTER the slash (or after turn 2 while the counter is over-counted by the slash) would hang. The recipe drives turn 2 with a generous trailing sleep instead of a post-slash wait_turn (same compromise the pi synchronous-slash-command recipe makes).

sources.note: model_change.modelId and assistant message.model â†’ ev.ModelName via tailer.NormalizeModelName; extractPiContribution tags per-turn model
pi 0.70.0: /model <provider>/<id> exact match â†’ session.setModel() directly, no picker; only an ambiguous/empty term opens the selector UI

sources.ref: live tmux drive of pi 0.70.0 in a temp cwd
sources.note: one rollout file, two turns; rollout: model_change(gpt-5.5) â†’ assistant(gpt-5.5,stop) â†’ model_change(gpt-5.4-mini) â†’ assistant(gpt-5.4-mini,stop). No new session on switch.
replay-summary model_name is environment-dependent â€” assert presence/change, not a hardcoded value

preconditions: pi CLI on PATH and authenticated (pi runs against its configured models out-of-band)
tmux available â€” the interactive driver runs pi in a tmux REPL so it can send two prompts to one long-lived session, switch models between them, and tear it down cleanly
irrlichd is recording â€” either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record

TWO models authenticated on the account: model A is pi's boot default (openai-codex/gpt-5.5 in the captured recording) and model B is a distinct switch target (openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini). Both must resolve to a UNIQUE exact match for the canonical provider/id form â€” if either model is unavailable, the /model switch silently falls back to the interactive picker and the script-driven switch fails.

The concrete model values are environment-dependent (issue #440): the durable assertion is that the model CHANGES between turns and a non-empty model_name (model B) is present at session end, NOT a hardcoded pair. The recipe pins both for determinism.
setup: The interactive driver (drive-pi-interactive.sh) launches pi in a fresh per-run cwd inside a tmux session and waits for the REPL prompt

pi writes one JSONL rollout file per session under ~/.pi/agent/sessions/<cwd-slug>/ on launch (FilesUnderRoot, non-lazy); the driver's end-of-script resolve_transcript fallback binds the UUID from line 1 (no wait_turn is used â€” see notes)

FIRST slash `/model openai-codex/gpt-5.5` PINS model A deterministically: pi persists the last-used model as ~/.pi/agent/settings.json defaultModel, so the boot model varies run-to-run; pinning A up front makes turn 1 reliably attributed to A regardless of what the previous session left as the default. This is the load-bearing determinism fix.

Each `/model <provider>/<id>` uses the CANONICAL provider/id form so pi's handleModelCommand resolves an exact single-model match and calls session.setModel() DIRECTLY â€” no arrow-key picker, no `keys` step. pi writes a model_change(modelId) line to the rollout (parsed for ModelName, no turn produced)

Turn 1 runs on model A (gpt-5.5); the SECOND slash `/model openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini` switches to model B; turn 2 runs on model B. message.model = A then B, so MergeMetrics' latest-non-unknown-wins promotes SessionMetrics.ModelName to B by t2_end and TotalTokens accumulates across both models

Both turns are driven by a fixed trailing sleep rather than wait_turn (the leading /model pin would desync the wait_turn counter â€” see notes); a one-word reply completes well within the 18s window (verified live)
After both turns + trailing sleep the driver kills the tmux session once â€” pi exits and the daemon emits a SINGLE process_exited for the rollout session (a model switch does NOT mint a new session)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: turn-one
Reply with exactly the word: turn-two

verify: events.jsonl shows two readyâ†’workingâ†’ready arcs on the SAME rollout session_id (turn 1 on model A, turn 2 on model B); the interactive REPL returns to ready after each, unlike headless pi --print which exits

events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered bound to the rollout session_id (a transient proc-<PID> pre-session may also appear born ready at launch; the turns and pid_discovered land on the UUID-keyed rollout session)
the /model slash produces NO additional working/waiting transition between t1_end and t2_start â€” it is a direct setModel() with no LLM round-trip and writes only a model_change line
events.jsonl contains exactly one process_exited on the rollout session at REPL teardown (a model switch does NOT create a second session)
no state_transition to waiting appears for the rollout session at any point; final live state is ready before teardown

the .replay.json.golden carries summary.model_name == model B (gpt-5.4-mini, latest-wins) â€” NOT model A and NOT 'unknown'; this is where the model Aâ†’B switch is asserted, since the events.jsonl phase DSL has no model field. The concrete value is environment-dependent (issue #440); assert change + presence, not a hardcoded string
recipe.notes: CRITICAL FIRST CHECK RESULT (recordability): pi 0.70.0's `/model <provider>/<id>` accepts an INLINE ARGUMENT â€” handleModelCommand â†’ findExactModelMatch â†’ session.setModel() switches DIRECTLY with no arrow-key picker when the reference resolves to exactly ONE model (dist/modes/interactive/interactive-mode.js ~L3216, dist/core/model-resolver.js findExactModelReferenceMatch ~L53). Only an empty/ambiguous term opens the selector UI. So the switch is sendable via the existing `slash` step in SCRIPT mode â€” NO driver_gap (no `keys` primitive needed, unlike the claudecode/codex picker recipes for this same scenario). MUST use the full canonical provider/id form (e.g. openai-codex/gpt-5.5, openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini); a bare fuzzy term like `mini` may match multiple models and fall back to the picker. WHY a LEADING /model pin (gpt-5.5) before turn 1: pi PERSISTS the last-used model as ~/.pi/agent/settings.json defaultModel, so a bare `pi` launch boots on whatever the previous session left behind â€” NON-deterministic. The first recording attempt booted on gpt-5.4-mini (left over from a probe) and 'switched' to gpt-5.4-mini â†’ NO observable model change. Pinning model A explicitly at the top makes the Aâ†’B switch a real change every run regardless of the persisted default. This is the load-bearing determinism fix. WHY NO wait_turn anywhere (both turns driven by trailing sleeps): the driver's `slash` step bumps the expected-turn counter exactly like `send`, but a /model switch produces a model_change line with NO assistant turn (turn_count only counts stopReason==stop). With a leading /model pin, EXPECTED is already 1 before turn 1, so a turn-1 wait_turn would block waiting for countâ‰Ą2 while only 1 turn exists â†’ driver timeout. The grammar has no 'slash that doesn't bump' primitive, so the recipe drops wait_turn entirely and lets a fixed 18s trailing sleep cover each one-word reply (completes well within 18s, verified live); the driver's end-of-script resolve_transcript fallback still binds the UUID. Same family of compromise as the pi synchronous-slash-command recipe (slash + sleep, no post-slash wait_turn). WHY the 2s/3s sleeps after each slash: they let the model_change line land and the classifier settle before the next prompt. The model lives on each assistant message.model (parser.go L103-105) and on the model_change line at modelId (L39-44), both normalized through tailer.NormalizeModelName into ev.ModelName â†’ SessionMetrics.ModelName (latest-non-unknown-wins). The model Aâ†’B assertion is NOT expressible in the events.jsonl phase DSL (no model field in the validator Phase struct), so expected.jsonl asserts only the two-turn lifecycle skeleton (readyâ†’workingâ†’ready twice on one rollout, no waiting, single process_exited) and the model switch is asserted at summary.model_name in the .replay.json.golden (which replay-fixtures.sh diffs: it pins model B) plus the pi parser unit tests. C7/SESSION-IDENTITY: the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch; session_birth (which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s') legitimately matches it. t1_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording (necessarily the rollout â€” the pre-session never goes working); t1_end/t2_start/t2_end, pid_bind, and teardown then pin to t1_start's session via same_session_as. What differs between runs: token counts/cost, rollout UUID/PID/timestamps, assistant wording (pinned with 'Reply with exactly the word: turn-one/turn-two'), and pi may flush a single turn as more than one workingâ†’ready burst (spec asserts â‰Ą2 arcs that reset cleanly, not an exact count). What must NOT differ: model A (gpt-5.5) on turn 1 and model B (gpt-5.4-mini) on turn 2 (pinned, so deterministic despite #440's general env-dependence note), two workingâ†’ready arcs on ONE rollout session, summary.model_name == model B in the golden, and a single process_exited at teardown. NOTE: the agent-scenarios-coverage.json cell still reads irrlicht_observes:no with a stale 'no model change detection between turns' note that predates the pi parser's model_change handling; this recipe + the committed assessment.json reflect the yes/yes reassessment (0.80). Surfacing the matrix drift here per the skill's 'don't edit the coverage matrix' rule â€” a maintainer should flip the cell.
